flashcards.front: What is a Tree data structure?
flashcards.back: A tree is a non-linear hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes connected by edges, where data is organized in a branching structure unlike linear arrangements.

flashcards.front: What is the Root node in a tree?
flashcards.back: The root is the topmost node of a tree that has no parent. It is the starting point from which all other nodes are reachable via edges.

flashcards.front: What is a Leaf node (terminal node)?
flashcards.back: A leaf node is a node with no children (degree = 0). It is also called a terminal node and appears at the bottommost level of the tree.
A parent node is a node that has one or more child nodes directly connected below it. A child node is directly connected to a parent node above it.
Sibling nodes are nodes that share the same parent. They are at the same level and have the same immediate ancestor.

flashcards.front: What is the Degree of a node?
flashcards.back: The degree of a node is the total number of children it has. A leaf node has degree 0, while the root can have degree equal to the number of its children.

flashcards.front: What is the Height of a tree?
flashcards.back: The height of a tree is the length of the longest path from the root to a leaf node. It measures the total number of levels in the tree.

flashcards.front: What is the Depth of a node?
flashcards.back: The depth of a node is the length of the path from the root to that particular node. The root has depth 0.

flashcards.front: What is the Level of a node?
flashcards.back: The level of a node is its depth plus 1. The root is at level 1, its children at level 2, and so on.
A binary tree is a tree data structure where each node can have at most two children, commonly referred to as the left child and right child.

flashcards.front: What is a Full (Strictly) Binary Tree?
flashcards.back: A full binary tree is a tree where every node has either 0 or exactly 2 children. No node has exactly 1 child.

flashcards.front: What is a Complete Binary Tree?
flashcards.back: A complete binary tree is a tree where all levels except possibly the last are completely filled, and all nodes are as far left as possible.

flashcards.front: What is a Binary Search Tree (BST)?
flashcards.back: A BST is a binary tree where left child contains values less than parent, right child contains values greater than parent, and this property holds recursively.

flashcards.front: How many edges does a tree with n nodes have?
flashcards.back: A tree with n nodes always has exactly (n-1) edges. This is because trees are connected acyclic graphs.
A subtree is a portion of a tree that can be viewed as a complete tree itself, consisting of a node and all its descendants.
